Features

Everything you need.
Nothing you don't.

Built for analysts who demand speed, precision, and intelligence — not a learning curve.

AI Signal Engine
Multi-agent AI scans 100M+ securities generating BUY/HOLD/SELL signals with confidence scores updated in real time.
NewsJudge
Distills hundreds of headlines into actionable impact scores. See which news moves which stocks — before the crowd reacts.
AI Command Center
10 specialized AI agents — Market Intel, Risk, Sentiment, Prediction — working in parallel. Institutional analysis on demand.
Portfolio Tracker
Real-time P&L, allocation analysis, and AI-generated rebalancing suggestions. Tracks your performance down to the cent.
Scenario Engine
Model any market scenario — rate hikes, earnings surprises, geopolitical events — and see projected portfolio impact instantly.
Backtesting
Validate your strategy against historical data. Sharpe ratio, max drawdown, win rate — all in seconds. No code required.
Macro Dashboard
Fed rates, yield curves, CPI, and global macro indicators — all live, all in context, all with AI commentary.
Universe Browser
Browse and analyze 100M+ equities across 100+ exchanges with real-time data via Massive Market Data integration.
AI Weekly Reports
Auto-generated portfolio performance reports every week. Deep analysis, not just numbers — your personal analyst on retainer.

Up and running
in 60 seconds.

Create your account
Sign up with your email — no credit card, no setup. Unlimited full access starts immediately.

Get your edge
Explore live signals, run scenarios, command your AI agents. Make every decision with conviction.

About

One person. One mission.
Better data for everyone.

Quant Entelloq was born from a simple frustration — the best financial intelligence tools were locked behind six-figure enterprise contracts. That needed to change.

By combining AI reasoning, real-time market signals, and thoughtful design, Quant Entelloq gives every serious decision-maker the same analytical edge that institutions take for granted.

DP
Darsh Prasad
Founder
Building intelligent data systems that drive smarter, faster, and more confident decisions at scale.
100M+
Securities
100+
Exchanges
24/7
AI Engine
FAQ

Common questions,
direct answers.

Is Quant Entelloq really free?
Yes — no credit card required. Create an account and get immediate, unlimited access to all features including the AI Command Center, NewsJudge, Macro Dashboard, Universe Browser, and Portfolio Tracker. No tiers, no limits.
How is this different from Bloomberg or Yahoo Finance?
Bloomberg costs $24,000/year. Yahoo Finance is a news aggregator. Quant Entelloq is a reasoning system — it analyzes data with 10 specialized AI agents and delivers clear, actionable conclusions in plain language. No data lookup. Actual intelligence.
How accurate are the AI signals?
Every signal includes a confidence score so you always know how strongly the AI stands behind its conclusion. Our multi-agent ensemble reduces individual model bias by requiring convergence across independent perspectives before issuing high-confidence calls.
Is my financial data secure?
Absolutely. Passwords are bcrypt-hashed (cost factor 12). Sessions use httpOnly cookies inaccessible to JavaScript. No brokerage credentials are ever handled by Quant Entelloq. AI conversations are not persisted beyond the session.
What AI models power the platform?
Quant Entelloq runs on frontier LLMs including LLaMA 3.3 70B via Groq for sub-second streaming responses. The multi-agent architecture deploys 10 specialized AI personas — Market Intel, Risk, Sentiment, Quant — each independently analyzing your query before synthesizing a conclusion.

⚠️ Failure Handling Matrix
Data Feed Outage
Detected by Ingestor missed-heartbeat > 30s. Falls back to cached snapshot & flags signals as "stale". Auto-recovers when feed restored.
LLM Rate Limit
Reasoner queues request, retries with exponential backoff (200ms → 1.6s → 12s), downgrades to template response after 3 failures.
Risk Validator Veto
Signal flagged but not blocked. User sees confidence downgraded by 30% and risk warning banner in output.
Output Router Failure
Dead-letter queue captures undelivered messages. Daily cron retries; user sees "Delivery delayed" in alert log with reason code.

Interpretation Guide
Sharpe > 1 is good, > 2 is excellent. Sortino penalizes only downside deviations — higher is better. VaR 95% means on 5% of days you can expect to lose at least this much. CVaR is the average loss in those worst 5% of days (also called Expected Shortfall). Calmar > 1 means the return justifies the drawdown risk. Omega > 1 means gains outweigh losses relative to the threshold. Negative skewness = fat left tail (crash risk). Kurtosis > 0 = fat tails vs normal.
Asset Inputs (Annual Expected Return % · Annual Volatility %)

Volatility %
Asset 4 Name
Exp. Return %
Volatility %
Avg Pairwise Correlation
Simulated Portfolios
Modern Portfolio Theory
Each dot is a randomly weighted portfolio. The left-edge curve is the Efficient Frontier — no portfolio to the left of it is achievable. Min Variance = lowest possible risk. Max Sharpe = best risk-adjusted return (tangency portfolio). Real-world use: allocate capital along the frontier based on your risk tolerance. Diversification reduces portfolio volatility below the weighted average of individual asset vols when correlations < 1.
Black-Scholes Inputs
Spot Price (S)
Strike Price (K)
Days to Expiry
Risk-Free Rate (%)
Implied Vol (%)
Option Type
Option Premium
—
Call: — Put: — d1: — d2: —
The Greeks
Delta (Δ)
—
Sensitivity to spot price
Gamma (Γ)
—
Rate of delta change
Theta (Θ)
—
Daily time decay ($)
Vega (ν)
—
Sensitivity per 1% vol
Rho (ρ)
—
Sensitivity per 1% rate
P&L at Expiration
Greeks Interpretation
Delta: How much the option price moves per $1 move in spot. Call delta 0–1, put delta −1–0. Gamma: How fast delta changes — high near expiry and ATM. Theta: Time decay per day — options lose value as time passes (negative for buyers). Vega: Value change per 1% increase in implied volatility — long options benefit from vol spikes. Rho: Interest rate sensitivity — minor for short-dated options.

Prob. of Loss
—
Prob. 2× Money
—
Geometric Brownian Motion Simulation
Each path simulates one possible future using GBM: dS = μS·dt + σS·dW. The cone shows probabilistic spread — not a prediction. The median (P50) is the most likely outcome; the fan represents uncertainty that grows with time. Long-term investing reduces risk of loss as compounding overcomes short-term volatility. Results assume constant parameters — real markets have regime changes, tail events, and correlation breaks.
Returns Input
GARCH(1,1) Parameters
ω (omega)
—
Long-run variance floor
α (alpha)
—
ARCH effect (shock impact)
β (beta)
—
GARCH effect (persistence)
α + β (persistence)
—
<1 = stationary
Volatility Statistics
Long-Run Vol (ann.)
—
Current Cond. Vol
—
Shock Half-Life
—
days for vol shock to halve
10-Day Vol Forecast
5-Day Forecast
—
10-Day Forecast
—
GARCH Model Explained
GARCH(1,1) models time-varying volatility: σ²ₜ = ω + α·ε²ₜ₋₁ + β·σ²ₜ₋₁. Alpha captures how quickly vol reacts to shocks (ARCH effect). Beta measures persistence — how long high vol lasts. α + β close to 1 means volatility is highly persistent (common in equity markets). The half-life tells you how many days it takes for a volatility shock to decay by half. Long-run vol is where volatility reverts over time. Practical use: VaR, option pricing, risk management, position sizing.
Returns Input
Rolling Window (days)
Distribution Statistics
Daily Mean Return
—
Daily Std Dev
—
Skewness
—
<0 = left-tailed (crash risk)
Excess Kurtosis
—
>0 = fat tails vs normal
Mean Reversion + Normality
Jarque-Bera Stat
—
Normality Test
—
MR Half-Life
—
(Ornstein-Uhlenbeck)
ACF Lag-1 / Lag-5
— / —
Rolling Z-Score
Autocorrelation Function (ACF)
Statistical Concepts
Skewness: Negative = crash risk (more extreme left tails). Kurtosis: Positive excess kurtosis = more extreme events than a normal distribution predicts — standard risk models will underestimate tail risk. Jarque-Bera: Tests for normality — reject if > 5.99 (p<0.05). Mean Reversion Half-Life: From the Ornstein-Uhlenbeck model — how many days for a deviation from mean to halve. Low = fast mean reversion, useful for pair trading. ACF: Autocorrelation — bars outside the yellow band indicate significant serial correlation (predictability).
Asset Universe (8 major asset classes)
Using empirically derived correlations based on 5-year historical data (2019–2024).
Market Regime
Correlation Heatmap
Diversification Analysis
Avg Pairwise Corr
—
Diversification Score
—
(100 = fully uncorrelated)
Highest Corr Pair
—
Lowest Corr Pair
—
Correlation in Portfolio Construction
Correlations near +1 mean assets move together — no diversification benefit. Correlations near 0 provide maximum diversification. Negative correlations (bonds vs stocks in normal regimes) act as portfolio hedges. Warning: Correlations are not stable — they spike toward 1 during market crises (correlation breakdown), exactly when diversification is most needed. The crisis regime shows how correlations shift during stress events. Always stress-test correlation assumptions.
Position Sizing Calculator
Kelly Criterion gives the theoretically optimal fraction of capital to risk on each trade to maximize long-run geometric growth.

Kelly Fractions

—

—

—

—
Expected value / trade
Dollar Amounts (Portfolio: $100,000)
Full Kelly Position
—
Half Kelly Position
—
Quarter Kelly Position
—
Capped Position
—
Growth Analysis
Kelly Criterion — Theory & Practice
Discrete Kelly: f* = (b·p − q) / b, where b = Win/Loss ratio, p = win rate, q = 1 − p. A negative result means the strategy has negative expected value — don't trade it.

Continuous Kelly: f* = μ / σ² (log-optimal portfolio fraction). Derived from maximizing E[ln(1 + f·r)].

Caution: Full Kelly maximizes long-run growth but causes extreme volatility — drawdowns of 50%+ are common. Half Kelly (f*/2) is the professional standard: it achieves ~75% of maximum growth with far less variance. Over-betting (f > Kelly) is strictly worse than under-betting in expectation. Kelly assumes accurate probability estimates — overconfidence leads to ruin.

🚀 Welcome to Quant Entelloq
The world is run by
financial intelligence.
Every company you see — Apple, Tesla, Google — started as an idea that people believed in enough to invest money. The stock market is where that belief is measured, every single second. This 5-scene experience will show you how it all works — no experience required.
LIVE MARKET PRICES — ALWAYS MOVING

🕐 5 minutes · 5 scenes · zero experience needed
📋 Scene 1 of 5 — Ownership
Owning a stock means
owning a piece of a company.
When Apple needs money to build iPhones and hire engineers, they sell ownership slices of the company. Each slice is called a share. Buy one share and you literally own a tiny fraction of Apple's patents, buildings, profits, and future.
Ownership Simulator
Choose a company above to see what you'd own →

YOUR STAKE
0%
INVESTMENT VALUE
$0
⚡ Scene 2 of 5 — Price Movement
Prices move because
of human emotion.
When good news hits — earnings beat, new product launch, CEO praised — more people want to buy. Demand rises, price rises. Bad news does the opposite. Fear and greed drive prices every day. Understanding this is your first superpower.
📰 News Impact Game — Predict the market reaction:
📈 Scene 3 of 5 — Compounding
Wealth is built through
compounding time.
A business that grows 20% per year doubles in size every 3.6 years. After 20 years, your $1,000 becomes $38,337. This is why the best investors hold great companies for decades — not days. Time is the most powerful variable in investing.

📉 Scene 4 of 5 — Market Cycles
Markets crash. Markets recover.
This cycle never ends.
Every market crash in history was followed by a recovery. Every bubble was preceded by euphoria. The investor who understands the emotional cycle — and does the opposite of the crowd — wins. Panic sells low. Wisdom buys low.

🏢
What Is a Stock?
Own a piece of Apple, Tesla, or any company on Earth
▼
When a company needs money to grow, it sells small pieces of itself called shares. Buy one share of Apple and you literally own a fraction of Apple's factories, patents, and future profits. That's a stock.
Ownership Simulator
Choose a company:

02
⚡
Why Do Prices Move?
Supply, demand, and the stories investors tell themselves
▼
Stock prices move because of supply and demand. More buyers than sellers → price rises. More sellers → price falls. But what drives buyers and sellers? News, earnings, emotions, and expectations about the future.
News Impact Simulator
A news headline drops. What happens to the stock?
03
📈
How Companies Grow
Revenue, profit, and the magic of compounding
▼
A company that grows 20% per year doubles in size every ~3.6 years. That's the power of compounding. The stock price follows earnings over time. Great businesses make shareholders very wealthy — over decades, not overnight.
Compounding Growth Visualizer

Why Markets Crash & Rise
Fear, greed, and the emotional cycle that repeats forever
▼
Markets move in cycles driven by human emotion. Panic causes crashes — everyone sells at the worst time. Optimism drives rallies — everyone buys near the top. The investor who understands this cycle and does the opposite of the crowd wins.
Market Psychology Cycle

Quant Entelloq is an AI-powered research tool only. Nothing on this platform constitutes financial advice, investment recommendations, or an offer to buy or sell any security. We use local storage
Quant Entelloq stores your preferences, API keys, and session data locally in your browser — nothing is sent to our servers. No tracking cookies are used.
Terms of Service
Quant Entelloq by Entelloq Networks · Last updated May 2026

1. Acceptance of Terms

By accessing or using Quant Entelloq ("the Platform"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service ("Terms"). If you do not agree, do not use the Platform.

2. Nature of the Service

Quant Entelloq is an AI-powered financial research and information platform. The Platform provides market data, AI-generated analysis, news aggregation, and portfolio tracking tools for informational and educational purposes only. Nothing on this Platform constitutes:

  • Financial advice or investment recommendations
  • An offer, solicitation, or invitation to buy or sell any security
  • Legal, tax, or accounting advice
  • Personalized investment guidance

3. Financial Risk Disclaimer

TRADING AND INVESTING INVOLVE SUBSTANTIAL RISK OF LOSS. YOU MAY LOSE ALL OR MORE THAN YOUR INITIAL INVESTMENT. PAST PERFORMANCE IS NOT INDICATIVE OF FUTURE RESULTS.

Entelloq Networks, its officers, directors, employees, agents, and affiliates are NOT responsible, liable, or accountable for any trading or investment losses you may incur as a result of using, relying on, or referencing any content, data, signal, analysis, or AI output provided by the Platform.

4. No Regulatory Registration

Quant Entelloq and Entelloq Networks are not registered investment advisors, broker-dealers, or financial institutions with any regulatory authority including SEBI, SEC, FCA, ASIC, or any other jurisdiction's financial regulator. Use of this Platform does not create any advisory or fiduciary relationship.

5. AI-Generated Content

The Platform uses large language models (including Anthropic's Claude) to generate analysis. AI outputs may be inaccurate, incomplete, outdated, or hallucinated. Always independently verify all information before making any financial decision.

6. Data Accuracy

Market data, news, and financial figures may be delayed, simulated, or sourced from third-party providers. Entelloq Networks makes no warranty of accuracy, completeness, or timeliness of any data displayed.

7. Intellectual Property

All Platform code, design, branding, and content are the property of Entelloq Networks. You may not copy, redistribute, or create derivative works without express written permission.

8. Limitation of Liability

TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, ENTELLOQ NETWORKS SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES, INCLUDING LOSS OF PROFITS, LOSS OF DATA, OR FINANCIAL LOSSES ARISING FROM YOUR USE OF THE PLATFORM.

9. Changes to Terms

We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. Continued use of the Platform constitutes acceptance of updated Terms.

10. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by applicable law. Any disputes shall be resolved through binding arbitration.

BY USING QUANT ENTELLOQ, YOU ACCEPT FULL RESPONSIBILITY FOR ALL TRADING DECISIONS AND THEIR OUTCOMES. ENTELLOQ NETWORKS IS NOT RESPONSIBLE FOR YOUR LOSSES.
Privacy Policy
Quant Entelloq by Entelloq Networks · Last updated May 2026

1. Data We Collect

Quant Entelloq is a server-assisted application. When you create an account, the following data is stored securely on our servers:

  • Your email address and display name
  • A hashed (bcrypt) version of your password — your plaintext password is never stored
  • Your AI query usage (for internal analytics only)
  • Account creation timestamp

The following data is stored only in your browser's localStorage and is never transmitted to our servers:

  • Portfolio positions and preferences you enter
  • Onboarding selections (experience level, risk tolerance)
  • UI preferences (theme, country, settings)
  • Market data API key (Massive Market Data)

2. AI Queries

When you use AI features, your messages are routed through our secure backend to Groq's API. We do not store the content of your AI conversations. Please review Groq's Privacy Policy.

Market data is fetched from Massive Market Data using your own API key, directly from your browser. We do not proxy or log these requests. Please review Massive Market Data's Privacy Policy.

3. Authentication & Cookies

Session authentication uses a secure, httpOnly cookie (qz_token) containing a signed JWT. This cookie is never accessible to JavaScript and is automatically cleared on logout. It expires after 7 days. We do not use tracking cookies, third-party analytics, or advertising scripts.

4. Data Sharing

We do not sell, share, or rent your personal data. Your data is only shared with Groq (for AI inference) as described above. All other processing remains within our infrastructure.

5. Data Retention & Deletion

Your account data is retained on our servers for as long as your account exists. To request deletion of your account and all associated server-side data, contact us at the address below. Browser-local data (portfolio, preferences) can be cleared at any time via your browser's developer tools.

6. Security

Passwords are hashed with bcrypt (cost factor 12) and never stored in plaintext. Session tokens are stored exclusively in httpOnly cookies to prevent XSS-based token theft. Our server enforces rate limiting, input validation, and firewall rules to protect against common attacks. We recommend not using this Platform on shared or public computers.

7. Children's Privacy

This Platform is intended for users 18 years of age and older. It is not designed for or directed at minors.

8. Changes to This Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy periodically. The date at the top reflects the last revision. Continued use of the Platform constitutes acceptance.

9. Contact

For privacy inquiries or data deletion requests, contact: privacy@entelloqnetworks.com
